Defendant William Murphy'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIAM MURPHY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT FOR EQUITABLE RELIEF AND DAMAGES (C.C.P. Sec. 425.16).
APPEARANCES: James M. Millar, Esq. of Millar & Associates, APLC 981-8100 for pltf & opposing party; John P. Baba, Esq. of Wiegel & Fried, LLP 552-8230 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The motion is denied. Plaintiff sues for imposition of a trust regarding the subject property. The now deleted paragraph 29 does not alter the true character of the action. Moreover, paragraph 29 is moot as of April 1, 2009. In addition, the Court ruled that defendant shall file a response to the complaint by 04/27/09. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/QLK) JUDGE: QUENTIN L. KOPP, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
